_comment: Curated OTC PRN (as-needed) dosing DEFAULTS (issue #798). The medication-info / food-drug-interactions pattern: a committed, human-reviewable, CITED dataset keyed by RxNorm INGREDIENT CUI with a name/synonym fallback (#279). Every value is a public OTC Drug Facts label figure — NOT medical advice. The app only PRE-FILLS these onto the med form as a suggestion the user explicitly confirms/edits; nothing here is applied silently. `adult.minIntervalHours`/`adult.maxDailyCount` pre-fill the redose-notice fields; `pediatric.bands` reproduce the label's weight-band chart (mg per weight band) for informational lookup — NEVER a mg/kg computation. `pediatric.minAgeMonths` is a hard age GATE rendered as the label's own 'ask a doctor' refusal, not a dose. ASPIRIN has NO pediatric entry (Reye's syndrome) — the dataset structurally omits it, pinned by a dataset test. Bands carry an inclusive lower bound (minLbs) only; the lookup picks the highest band whose minLbs <= the child's weight, so a weight between two label bands lands conservatively on the LOWER (lower-dose) band, and a weight below the smallest band is a refusal.

ingredients.label: Ibuprofen

pediatric.ageGateText: For children under 6 months, ask a doctor before use (per the product label).

formulations.label: Infants' oral suspension drops (50 mg / 1.25 mL)

ingredients.source: OTC Drug Facts label — ibuprofen oral suspension (children's), U.S. FDA.

ingredients.label: Acetaminophen

pediatric.ageGateText: For children under 12 weeks (about 3 months), ask a doctor before use (per the product label).

ingredients.source: OTC Drug Facts label — acetaminophen oral suspension (children's), U.S. FDA.

ingredients.label: Aspirin
ingredients.source: OTC Drug Facts label — aspirin tablets, U.S. FDA. NO pediatric entry: aspirin is not given to children/teens (Reye's syndrome) — omitted by design.

ingredients.label: Naproxen sodium
ingredients.source: OTC Drug Facts label — naproxen sodium tablets, U.S. FDA. Not for children under 12 without a doctor — no pediatric weight-band entry.

ingredients.label: Diphenhydramine
ingredients.source: OTC Drug Facts label — diphenhydramine HCl (antihistamine), U.S. FDA. The children's label doses by AGE, not weight — no weight-band table here (weight bands would misrepresent an age-based label).
